course_title: Project Management for Tourism and Events

learning_outcomes.description: Discuss the theoretical and practical components of project planning and operations
Use international case studies to demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of event design principles
Develop operational plans to strategically facilitate effective and efficient planning and management of events and tourism-related projects
Critically engage with issues of risk management, workforce, and logistics
Work effectively in teams to identify and propose solutions for real-world tourism and events operational issues

assessments.task_description:
• Three non-cumulative review quizzes (10% each) will be administered throughout the semester that will cover the course lecture material, readings, and the tutorial activities. 
  • 60 minutes will be allowed to complete each quiz between 8am and 8pm on the scheduled date.
  • Each quiz will consist of 10 questions consisting of an array of question types.
  • The questions will primarily require you to individually complete the problem based learning activities from our weekly seminars. 
  • Question types will include open response, submission of files, drawing figures, constructing charts, multiple choice, true/false and/or short answer questions drawn from the class content from the preceding weeks of content. 
  • All quizzes will be completed fully online via Inspera Assessment. Inspera Assessment provides additional functionality to complement the existing suite of digital learning tools at UQ with a greater range of question types, accessibility provisions, and non-English languages.
  • A formative practice quiz to allow students to gain familiarity with the types of questions to be asked will be available from the start of the course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will work in teams of four to six students to develop an event tourism proposal for a real client from the Brisbane business community. In week 4 of the semester, the client will provide event tourism briefs directly to the class. From these briefs you will:

  • Pitch your initial ideas in response to the brief (Elevator Pitch, Assessment 2 – individual)
  • Present your Client Event Tourism Proposal to the client (Assessment 3 – team)
  • Reflect on your experience working on the brief (Assessment 4 – individual)


For assessment 2, Elevator Pitch:

  • Students work individually to pitch their ideas in response to the client’s event tourism briefs as a scaffolded assessment for the Client Event Tourism Proposal.
  • The pitch will be delivered individually, live in class, during Week 6.
  • From the pitch, feedback will be provided by the class and teaching team to help refine and improve each student's ideas for the proposal. 
  • The elevator pitch will be 2-3 minutes maximum (strictly enforced).
  • You are required to attend all pitches and provide feedback to your peers (just as they will be providing feedback for you). 
  • The results of the elevator pitch and feedback provided will help teams shape and refine their ideas for the Team-Based Client Event Tourism Proposal due during the exam period.
  • A detailed presentation schedule will be provided to the class via Blackboard.
  • Please note: The pitch will be recorded for marking purposes.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Exam week 1 - Exam week 2

Presentations will be scheduled during exam period. Date to be released during the term as scheduled by the the UQ Examinations Office.
assessments.task_description:
Students will work in teams of four to six throughout the semester to develop an Event Tourism Proposal for a real client from the Brisbane business community.

In Week 4, the client will present a series of event tourism briefs directly to the class. Each team will select or be allocated a brief and develop a proposal addressing the client’s real-world problems and objectives. The proposal will incorporate key topics covered in the course, including:

  • Overview, purpose, concept, market, feasibility, timing
  • site selection and layout;
  • staffing and volunteer requirements;
  • staging and logistics;
  • risk management; and
  • financing and budgeting.

Teams must apply the project management tools developed throughout the semester, including Gantt charts, run sheets, work breakdown structures and site maps. The project will be completed in partnership with EventsAir, an industry-leading event management software platform. Students will receive training in EventsAir and use the platform to support the development of their proposals.

Teams will be formed by students, with assistance from the teaching staff, in Week 1. Students will remain in these teams throughout the semester.

At the end of the semester, each team will deliver its proposal directly to the client and teaching staff through:

  • a pitch of no more than 10 minutes;
  • a five-minute oral defence conducted through a question-and-answer session; and
  • a written proposal of no more than 15 single-sided pages.

All team members must contribute to the pitch and respond to questions during the oral defence.

Marks will reflect each student’s individual contribution to the assessment task. Students must complete confidential peer evaluations at both the middle and end of the semester. Individual grades will be calculated by weighting the team’s overall assessment score according to each student’s peer-evaluation score, including their self-evaluation.

Complete assessment instructions and marking criteria will be provided on Blackboard.

Students are encouraged to consult the following group-work resources:

  • UQ Study Skills: Group Work
  • Working in Teams: A Practical Guide

Please note that the presentation and oral defence will be recorded for marking purposes in accordance with UQ policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Live in-person client pitch and oral defence, with the written proposal and presentation materials submitted through Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: This assessment task requires you to individually write a 1,000–1,500-word reflective essay on your experience working on the Client Event Proposal Assessment from receiving the client brief to delivering the final proposal.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via Blackboard, will be subject to plagiarism and originality.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
